TextENGNew: ...The metallic taste of blood-soaked earth grated against the inside of my mouth.
...A taste like iron clung stubbornly to my tongue.
...I was lying on hard, damp earth. ...Here, inside a cramped hole.
I had been thrown into a suffocating miasma of sweat, dirt, and blood.
...It wasn't a deep hole by any means, yet I could never escape it.
...Ah, right. I'm dead. That's why I'm in a place like this.
A shock ran through the back of my neck, and immediately after, something hard struck me. A bat -- a metal bat. Satoshi's bat.
A huge hatchet, raised overhead at some point I hadn't noticed, bit into the back of my neck... I don't remember much after that.
At any rate, I was dead. I had been murdered.
In this damp, gloomy forest............... alone.
--Help me, they're going to kill me.
I could hear someone screaming. An earsplitting scream.
Satoko...
What was happening? I had no intention of killing Satoko.
After all... I was already dead.
Rena and Shion were watching me, laughing.
Both of them -- clothes drenched with sweat, caked in mud -- wore cold, cold smiles.
Hey, why are you looking at me with such cold eyes?
We're friends, aren't we? We did this together because we're friends, right? Comrades in arms, you might say.
Welcome me back warmly...
``Ahahaha, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200359. We were never friends.@k@vS18/02/160200360. ...How long are you going to keep dreaming?``
Rena laughed with her usual adorable face.
Not friends? What was she talking about...?
``Hau~, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200362. Why would you ask Rena something like that? Like that?``
``He's bombarding us with questions to keep us from asking any ourselves.@k@vS18/06/160600362. He'd sooner die than admit he did something wrong.``
I haven't done anything wrong.
Satoko...! I did all of this for you!
``Kei-chan, that's enough.@k@vS18/06/160600364. Because... you're already dead.``
Shion...! Rena...! Satoko...!
I jolted awake.
I had known it was a dream. From what point had I known?
...From the very beginning.
Because I wasn't dead. ...The one who did the killing... was me.
I conjured the image of Satoko's uncle, writhing in the dark forest.
The reddish-black color of blood. His face, split open like a pomegranate.
His pathetic back, curled up and scrambling to escape. His pitiable fingers, unable to fight back...
And me, putting every ounce of strength into the metal bat...
As those memories resurfaced with vivid clarity, bile rose in my stomach.
Last night felt like a distant memory from another era.
Even so, it was the truth of what happened to me last night.
Yet when I thought back on it... I felt as though there was a gaping hole in my heart.
...What am I thinking? Get it together, Keiichi Maebara!
I'm not alone. I have friends, don't I?
What reason do I have to cry?
...I set out to accomplish something, and I accomplished it perfectly, didn't I?
I should hold my head high and live proudly.
I accomplished what no one else could... I rescued a helpless girl named Satoko in the span of a single night...!
``Keiichi, hurry up or you'll be late!``
At Mom's voice, I scrambled to change clothes.
``Coming!``
I had reclaimed our 'rightful future.' Starting today, the normal, everyday routine would resume.
Today was the usual happy morning.
And Rena would be waiting for me at the usual spot, same as always.
I wolfed down a delicious breakfast as usual, stuffed myself as usual, and dashed out of the house as usual.
The sunlight gently illuminated the school building.
Children's voices echoed across the schoolyard, confirming that the peaceful everyday was still intact.
Once more, the dark, heavy, surreal events of yesterday felt like something from a distant world -- or maybe a dream.
Rena wasn't at the meeting place today either.
Neither was Mion. Feeling a slight unease about the everyday life I was supposed to have reclaimed, I headed to the classroom alone.
I wanted to see Satoko...
That was the only hope I had left to cling to.
...Satoko probably wasn't going to make it to school today.
But if she was here, I'd happily walk right into one of her traps today!
I threw the classroom door open with gusto.
But for better or worse, no eraser fell on my head.
``Kei-chan, good mooorning~!``
``Good morning, Mii-chan!@k@vS18/02/160200364. Today was duty day, right?@k@vS18/02/160200365. Rena completely forgot and went to the regular meeting spot!``
Rena breezed right past me and started chatting with Mion in an unexpectedly high-energy tone.
Rena...?
Something about Rena felt different from yesterday, and it unsettled me.
Why hadn't she come to the meeting place like usual?
...There was no particular reason, right?
...And walking right past me as if I wasn't there -- there was no particular reason for that either, right?
Perhaps sensing my anxious expression, Rena turned to face me properly and gave me a bright smile.
``Keiichi-kun, good morning!``
``Good morning...``
Before I could voice the question on my mind, Rena quickly averted her gaze and turned her smile back to Mion.
``Yesterday was so much fun, Mii-chan!``
I couldn't understand what Rena was thinking.
Was Rena... trying to betray me...?
Or perhaps the betrayal had already begun...?
Last night's events suddenly pressed in on me with stark reality.
...No, that's wrong! 'That dream' was just a dream -- not a prophecy, not a premonition!
``Ahaha! Kei-chan, you were way too hyped up last night~@k@vS18/03/160300133. You look like an empty cicada shell this morning.``
``You're one to talk, Mion!@k@vS18/01/160100506. You ate eight servings of yakisoba!``
I raised my voice to keep pace with the two of them.
We were at the shrine grounds last night! Rena and I were both there! We enjoyed the festival!
That alone... was the truth...!
``It was an eating contest! Of course I ate a lot!@k@vS18/03/160300135. I skipped lunch to prepare for it, you know?``
``But Keiichi-kun did his best too, right?@k@vS18/02/160200369. Even the takoyaki vendor took a liking to him!``
``Damn right!@k@vS18/01/160100508. Next year, I'll be the one making the takoyaki!``
``Mii~.@k@vS18/05/160500014. I think Keiichi should stick to being a salesman and leave the cooking to the professionals.``
``Why's that?``
``...Keiichi's cooking has a destructive potential rivaling thermonuclear weapons.``
``Nipah~``
``Hehehe!@k@vS18/03/160300137. This old man definitely doesn't want to eat Kei-chan's takoyaki~``
W-what~?! Hey, Rena! Cover fire, please!
``Now that I think about it, Rena's stomach hasn't been feeling too strong lately. ...Nope.``
That perfectly vague non-answer was a full-force rejection! Hey, come on!
Left hanging all by myself, laughter erupted around me.
All day, we spent every free moment excitedly trading stories about the festival.
It started to feel like an obligation to talk about the festival, and eventually I was the one bringing up trivial festival anecdotes.
This too was an event. ...The mandatory 'after the festival' event.
The epilogue had to be concluded without a hitch. No one could be allowed to dredge up details about that night later on.
Today, right here, I'd get everyone to pour it all out -- and that would be the end of it.
That night would be put to rest, once and for all.
And once the day was over, I'd pack up and head straight home.
I was genuinely exhausted.
...At the festival last night, when all my energy had gone into performing enthusiasm, there hadn't been a single moment of genuine enjoyment.
Every time I said 'last night was so much fun,' I had to scramble to reconcile the disconnect between my words and my feelings... and by the end of the day, I was utterly spent, body and soul.
I didn't want to talk to anyone else today. Rena probably felt the same.
``Keiichi-kun, shall we head home?``
Fortunately, Mion and Rika-chan were on duty today, so I started to leave the classroom alongside Rena.
``See you later, Kei-chan!@k@vS18/03/160300139. See you tomorrow, Rena!``
Mion waved at us while wiping the blackboard.
``Bye-bye~!@k@vS18/02/160200373. Good luck with your duties, Mii-chan~!``
``...Duty day isn't really something you need to 'good luck' at, is it?``
``Oh? So duty day is a breeze for you, Kei-chan?@k@vS18/03/160300141. This old man definitely couldn't handle it so easily!@k@vS18/03/160300142. I'd die trying!``
``...So, if it's that easy for you, want to trade duty days?``
``Not a chance!@k@vS18/01/160100512. Hey Rena, aren't you supposed to be good at duty day?@k@vS18/01/160100513. ...So yeah, leaving now, bye!``
``Aah! Keiichi-kun, wait~!@k@vS18/02/160200375. Also, Rena is totally not good at duty day~! Hau~...``
I scrambled out of the classroom, with Rena clattering after me.
Mion's booming laughter echoed all the way into the hallway.
``Keiichi...?``
Just before reaching the schoolyard, I nearly collided with Rika-chan, who was walking toward me carrying the class journal.
For some reason, she looked listless, her shoulders drooping.
``Rika-chan, what's wrong?``
Rena, walking beside me, called out to her in a gentle, concerned tone. But--
Rika-chan's gaze remained fixed on the ground.
She was staring intently at the dirt of the schoolyard.
...Staring at the dirt. Just staring.
``......Rika-chan?``
Rika-chan raised her head.
Her eyes were red and swollen. I realized then that Rika-chan had been crying alone somewhere, in secret.
``Rika-chan, what's going on?@k@vS18/01/160100516. Did something happen?``
``I'm fine... I just tripped, that's all...@k@vS18/05/160500020. See...?``
Rika-chan held out both hands. Her palms were smeared with dirt.
``Are you okay?@k@vS18/02/160200378. Want us to go to the nurse's office together? Hmm?``
Rika-chan shook her head in quick, trembling motions. Then... she seized my wrist in a tight grip.
``Rika-chan...?``
``I'm all right.@k@vS18/05/160500022. I'm all right...``
Rika-chan's voice was barely a whisper, on the verge of fading entirely. But she clearly wasn't all right.
``Rika-chan, you really don't look okay.``
``...Never mind about me.@k@vS18/05/160500024. ...More importantly, there is something I need to ask Keiichi and Rena.``
``...Something to ask?``
Rika-chan's grip on my wrist tightened.
At first, I thought she'd grabbed my wrist because she needed help.
Because she wanted me to take her to the nurse's office, or because she wanted someone to understand the pain from her fall...
...But I was wrong. The strength this supposedly fragile girl was pouring into my wrist was too powerful to resist.
...I understood then. She was gripping my wrist so I couldn't escape the question she was about to ask...
A sharp light from Rika-chan's eyes pierced straight into mine.
``I just... want to know...``
Rika-chan stared at me with detached, unflinching eyes.
There was no life in them -- like the eyes of a dead person. Her expression was so blank and resigned that nothing could be read from it...
And that, conversely, sent a chill through me.
She's asking about the festival last night, right? ...Right?
Why did she have to make that face just to ask about last night...?
``...W-what do you mean?``
Before I knew it, I had looked away from Rika-chan.
``Rika-chan... what is it you want to know? What is it?``
Rena was holding Rika-chan's gaze with admirable composure... but I could see the anxiety creeping into her profile.
``Keiichi, Rena...@k@vS18/05/160500028. Do you... know where Satoko went?``
My breath nearly stopped.
I stole a furtive glance at Rika-chan's face.
Her expression hadn't changed -- she was simply staring at us with those wide, unblinking eyes.
Her gaze was mystical, as if it could envelop my entire being... and at the same time, deeply unsettling.
``I want you to tell me.@k@vS18/05/160500030. Where did Satoko go...?``
Rika-chan repeated the question.
As if she were certain we knew the answer.
I couldn't even bring myself to look in her direction.
``...Keiichi.@k@vS18/05/160500032. On the night of the festival... did something happen?``
My body flinched.
And my wrist must have trembled too. ...Rika-chan would have felt the vibration. She must have sensed something.
``...Was it, perhaps, something bad...?``
Terror nearly buckled my knees.
Even if Rika-chan knew everything......... what could we do about it?!
``How about you, Rena...?``
Rika-chan slowly turned toward Rena.
``I-I don't know. Rena went straight to the festival and stayed there...``
``Straight... to the festival...?@k@vS18/05/160500036. Did you really stay at the festival... the entire time...?``
``...........................Satoko went to the festival...... and never came back.``
My heart hammered like an alarm bell.
Why was Rika-chan pressing us so relentlessly?
Relentlessly. ...Far too relentlessly.
Did Rika-chan know...?! About what happened last night...?
...Had she heard Satoko's screams?
If she knew... how much exactly did Rika-chan know?
``...Rena, please answer me.``
Rena reflexively took a step back.
My wrist was still caught in Rika-chan's grip. There was nothing I could do.
...How was I supposed to answer?
Rena stood still, her eyes lowered.
Rika-chan... turned those guileless eyes back and forth between us, and at last... let out a single sigh.
``I see...@k@vS18/05/160500041. If you don't know, then there's nothing more to ask...``
``I'm sorry I couldn't be more help...``
I exhaled as if I'd been saved.
Rika-chan released my wrist and spoke in a sorrowful voice.
``Satoko is missing.@k@vS18/05/160500043. I checked the Houjou house last night and again this morning, but it's completely quiet.@k@vS18/05/160500044. Nobody is there.``
...Rena and I couldn't find the words to respond.
``I'm sorry...@k@vS18/05/160500046. I got dirt on your hand, Keiichi...``
``Oh, r-right, it's fine.@k@vS18/01/160100522. It's just dirt -- it washes right off.``
``...Did you have dirt on your hands last night too, Keiichi?``
``Please wash them properly.@k@vS18/05/160500049. ...Keiichi and Rena wouldn't know anything about Satoko.@k@vS18/05/160500050. ...Of course not.``
``Nipah~``
Rika-chan broke into her usual smile, then bounced off toward the school building.
I couldn't bring myself to watch her go. I spun on my heel and started walking briskly in the opposite direction.
I pushed through the school gate at a near-jogging pace, without looking back.
Rena followed in silence.
Neither of us had any way of knowing that Rika-chan glanced back at us as we left...
Rena and I walked side by side.
Our conversation would trail off... then start again as if we'd just remembered something, then trail off...
While carefully skirting the one topic we both wanted to avoid, we exchanged hollow, absent-minded words until eventually the conversation died for good.
For a while, we walked in silence.
At last, we reached the fork where our paths diverged toward Rena's house.
``Hey, Keiichi-kun...@k@vS18/02/160200385. I'm a little worried, aren't you?``
Those were precisely the words I'd been wanting to say ever since we left the school gate.
``Worried about what...?``
I heard myself asking that, even though I already knew. Obviously, it was about Rika-chan just now.
Rika-chan knew something, didn't she? ...About something far too serious and decisive to brush off.
``About Satoko-chan.``
``Satoko...?``
I'd been entirely focused on what to do if Rika-chan had discovered the truth, so Rena's answer caught me off guard.
...Satoko?
Come to think of it, Rika-chan genuinely seemed not to know where Satoko was.
If she had known, she wouldn't have asked so insistently, and she wouldn't have looked so grief-stricken.
``If nobody's home at the Houjou house... don't you think that'll seem unnatural?``
Rena repeated.
``Someone in the village might notice soon.@k@vS18/02/160200389. That no one is living in that house anymore.@k@vS18/02/160200390. That both Teppei and Satoko are gone.``
It was true -- if more people started looking for Satoko, things could get complicated.
``I'll try calling Shion.``
``Rather than calling, let's go see Shii-chan in person.@k@vS18/02/160200392. Rena's been worried about what happened after we split up last night...``
``Good point...``
We agreed on a meeting place and rode our bikes to Okinomiya.
I flopped down the moment I got back to my room.
No desire to watch TV, no desire to read.
We'd gone to Angel Mort to see Shion, but she wasn't there. Apparently it was her day off.
I left a message asking her to call, and Rena and I headed home... but the phone still hadn't rung.
The clock had already passed 9 PM.
I'd tried calling Shion a while ago, but got no answer.
I left a message on her answering machine and had been sitting here, waiting for her call.
...Shion. If everything went smoothly last night, the least you could have done was call.
What had Shion been doing while Rena and I were whipping ourselves into a frenzy at the festival...?
In the end, Shion never showed up at the festival. Even though we'd promised to create each other's alibis by making a scene there together...
Thanks to the wild card of Satoko showing up, the plan had gone subtly off the rails.
I couldn't sit still. I paced back and forth across the living room.
Glaring at the silent phone, going to the bathroom, getting water...
``Keiichi, is something wrong?``
Mom finally called out to me, unable to watch any longer.
...No, it's nothing.
I muttered that and slunk back to my room.
What if something had happened to Shion...?
Anxiety reared its head at alarming speed.
Maybe she couldn't make contact because something had happened. ...What kind of something?
.........The curse of Oyashiro-sama.
That's absurd...! Why would the curse come for Shion...?!
I scoffed at the ridiculous thought.
But the very next thing I thought about was, once again, the curse.
...On the night of Watanagashi... one person dies... and one person vanishes...
The uncle was dead. He was this year's victim. ...The fifth year's curse fell on Teppei Houjou.
The dead: Satoko's uncle.
......Then who was this year's disappearance?
The phone jolted me upright.
``Kei-chan?``
The familiar voice flooded me with relief from the bottom of my heart.
``Shion!``
I could feel the strength draining from my entire body. ...She was alive. Shion was safe...!
``Kei-chan, are you all right?@k@vS18/06/160600367. I got your messages, so I called. What's going on?``
``What do you mean, 'what's going on'?@k@vS18/01/160100531. I've been worried sick about what happened after we split up!@k@vS18/01/160100532. You could've at least called!``
Shion was safe... that joy alone made my heart lighter and lighter.
I was so relieved it was just overthinking...! The idea that the curse had taken Shion was absurd from the start.
I guess the one with something wrong was me all along.
``S-sorry...@k@vS18/06/160600369. I made you worry, didn't I?``
``Yeah, you did!@k@vS18/01/160100534. More importantly, did you get home safely last night?@k@vS18/01/160100535. Rena and I went to the festival and made our alibi, so we're fine.``
``Yes, no problems whatsoever.``
``And what about Satoko...?@k@vS18/01/160100537. Where did you take her?``
``About that...``
I felt a sudden tension radiating from the receiver in my clenched hand.
``Did something happen...?``
``The police are apparently searching desperately for Teppei Houjou, who's been missing since last night.``
``...?! The police already know about his disappearance?!``
``Yes. Someone saw him head out on his motorcycle in the evening.@k@vS18/06/160600374. ...And the Houjou house has been completely empty since last night.``
...Things had progressed that far already...?!
I thought of the pitch-dark forest where I'd buried the uncle.
``So Shion, where did you take Satoko?``
``She's fine.@k@vS18/06/160600376. She's sleeping quietly in a place no one will find.@k@vS18/06/160600377. When the time is right, I'll bring her to you, Kei-chan.``
Shion stubbornly refused to reveal Satoko's location.
...Suspicion toward Shion began to well up inside me.
``...Why?@k@vS18/01/160100542. Why can't you tell me where Satoko is?``
``...Since she disappeared at the same time as her uncle, the police are apparently treating Satoko as a person of interest.``
Those words hit me like a bucket of ice water, stripping away my discomfort and replacing it with a creeping dread that made me shrink into myself.
The investigation had already reached that point...?! This pace was beyond anything we could have anticipated.
But why? Why would they launch such a large-scale investigation just because someone had been absent for a day or two?!
Why?! ...Could someone have seen something last night...?!
Or maybe...
I pressed my ear harder against the receiver.
Shion waited in silence for me to speak...
The word 'betrayal' flickered across my mind.
Not just anyone's... but... probably...
'...Did something bad happen last night?'
I remembered what Rika-chan had asked me after school.
I gingerly touched the wrist Rika-chan had gripped. It still felt like dirt was clinging to it, and a chill ran through me.
``Kei-chan, you've gone quiet. Are you okay?``
``Shion.@k@vS18/01/160100544. ...Can we meet tomorrow?``
On the other end of the line, I heard her catch her breath.
``Shion...?@k@vS18/01/160100546. Is something wrong?``
``Not particularly.@k@vS18/06/160600382. That's fine.``
``The Okinomiya library, then.@k@vS18/01/160100548. Rena will be coming too.``
``Rena-san as well?@k@vS18/06/160600384. Understood.@k@vS18/06/160600385. Tomorrow, then.``
I set the receiver down and hung up.
For some reason, my heart was pounding.
An anxiety that shouldn't have existed between Shion and me -- that must not exist -- was undeniably there.
There should have been nothing to fear... yet I couldn't shake this terrible premonition...
...It's fine. Once I meet Shion tomorrow, I'll surely realize it was all in my head.
Once tomorrow comes and Shion tells me clearly where Satoko is, all this anxiety will vanish in an instant.
I burrowed into my futon, desperate for tomorrow to arrive.
But my mind was too restless with worry, and sleep refused to come...
